text:
Your working directory is a checkout of the real yet-another-react-lightbox repository at the commit where the feature gap below needs closing. Implement the feature in the library source.

Context: this library renders a photo/video lightbox carousel. It ships a Zoom plugin that adds pinch/scroll/double-click zoom interactions to the currently displayed slide. Right now the Zoom plugin only ever recognizes image slides -- internally it decides whether to wrap a slide in its interactive zoom container purely by checking whether that slide is an image slide, with no way to opt any other kind of slide into zoom. Consumers can render arbitrary custom slide types through the lightbox's `render.slide` render-prop (keyed by a slide's `type` field, e.g. a custom map view, a video player, a PDF page, etc.), but none of those custom slide types can ever get zoom behavior, no matter what the consumer configures -- the plugin just renders them completely unwrapped, with no zoom UI, no pinch/scroll/double-click handling, nothing.

Feature request: add a way for a consumer to opt specific custom slide types into the same zoom wrapper/gesture handling that image slides already get. Concretely, the Zoom plugin's options object (the `zoom` prop passed to the lightbox) should grow two new optional properties:

- `supports`: a list of slide `type` strings. When the currently displayed slide's `type` is included in this list, the plugin should wrap it in the same interactive zoom container an image slide gets (so it participates in zoom in/out, pinch, scroll-to-zoom, double-click/double-tap, etc., exactly like an image slide does) -- even though the slide itself isn't an image slide. A slide whose `type` is not image and not listed in `supports` (or when `supports` is not configured at all) should continue to render completely unwrapped, exactly as today.

- `maxZoom`: controls how far a non-image slide can be zoomed in. For image slides the maximum zoom level is already computed automatically from the image's natural resolution vs. its displayed size, but that computation is meaningless for a non-image custom slide (there's no natural image resolution to compare against), so it needs its own configurable ceiling. `maxZoom` may be a plain number, or a function that receives the current slide and returns either a number or `undefined`; returning `undefined` (or omitting `maxZoom` entirely) should fall back to a sensible default maximum zoom level of 8x. This setting only affects non-image slides -- image slides keep using their existing resolution-based maximum zoom calculation unchanged.

Environment notes: dependencies are already installed (Node, npm). You can scope the existing Vitest suite to whatever file you're iterating on with `npx vitest run <path-to-file>`. Implement the feature in the library source; do not just add workarounds in test files.

text: I’ll trace the Zoom plugin’s slide eligibility and maximum-zoom calculation, then update the public option types, runtime behavior, and focused tests. I’ll preserve the existing image path and verify custom supported slides remain unwrapped by default.

input.command: /bin/bash -lc 'pwd && rg --files | head -80 && rg -n "maxZoom|Zoom|zoom" src test tests 2>/dev/null | head -160'
/bin/bash -lc "sed -n '300,345p' src/types.ts && sed -n '55,95p' src/types.ts && sed -n '1,150p' test/unit/test-utils.tsx && cat package.json"

text: The key coupling is broader than the render condition: the controller currently marks zoom disabled unless the current slide has an image source, and geometry returns a maximum of `1` when no image dimensions exist. I’m adapting those two paths so a supported custom slide gets a slide-sized zoom surface and a configurable ceiling, while image slides continue through the existing natural-resolution calculation.

input.command: /bin/bash -lc "rg -n \"maxZoomPixelRatio|supports:\" README.md docs website dev test src | head -120 2>/dev/null || true && sed -n '1,130p' src/index.ts && sed -n '1,100p' src/plugins/index.ts"

text: I’m going to add a small shared eligibility helper, expose the two new option types in the Zoom module declaration, and make custom-slide geometry use the controller’s displayed slide rectangle. The custom renderer remains the source of the slide content; the Zoom plugin only adds the established wrapper and controller behavior when explicitly opted in.

text: The focused suite confirms the behavior and found only a test assertion issue: the carousel intentionally preloads the same custom slide in three positions, so the custom renderer appears three times. I’m narrowing that assertion to presence rather than uniqueness, then running the full unit suite and lint/type checks available in the repository.

text: The full unit suite passes: 13 files and 78 tests. The repository’s type-test script cannot start because this checkout has no `dist` directory; that is a build prerequisite rather than a feature failure. I’m running the normal build now, which also validates declaration generation for the new Zoom option types.

text: The build caught the expected TypeScript narrowing edge: a `maxZoom` callback may return `undefined`, and that value needs to be coalesced before passing it to `Math.max`. I’m correcting that explicit fallback and rebuilding.

text:
Implemented Zoom support for custom slide types.

Changes:
- Added `zoom.supports` to opt custom slide types into zoom wrapping.
- Added `zoom.maxZoom` as a number or slide-based function.
- Defaults supported non-image slides to `8x`.
- Preserved image resolution-based max zoom behavior.
- Enabled zoom controller state and geometry for supported custom slides.
- Added regression tests for opt-in, opt-out, custom max zoom, and default max zoom.

Verification passed: build, type tests, full unit suite (78 tests), focused Zoom tests, formatting, and lint.
